proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the continuous covariates, feature_015 (range 30-90, mean 65) is positively associated with pfs_months and is the strongest single predictor of PFS in the cohort.
feature_014 (ordered 0/1/2) is monotonically and negatively associated with pfs_months: higher feature_014 levels predict shorter PFS, consistent with a performance-status- or stage-like marker.
Patients with feature_006='squamous' have shorter pfs_months than patients with feature_006='adenocarcinoma'.
Patients with feature_001='current' have shorter pfs_months than feature_001='former' or 'never' patients.

analyses.code:
from scipy import stats
stats.pearsonr(df['feature_015'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Pearson r=0.757 between feature_015 and pfs_months (p<1e-300). Mean PFS rises monotonically across feature_015 quintiles: Q1=1.14, Q2=2.42, Q3=3.37, Q4=4.36, Q5=5.92 months. Strongest single predictor identified.
Mean PFS by feature_014 level: 0 -> 4.33, 1 -> 3.19, 2 -> 2.15. ANOVA F=3141, p<1e-300. Strong monotonic decrease as feature_014 rises.
Mean PFS: adeno=3.67 (n=36099), squamous=2.81 (n=13901). t-test p<1e-300, ANOVA F=1522.5. Squamous patients have ~0.86 months shorter PFS.
Mean PFS by smoking status: current=2.99 (n=14974), former=3.61 (n=27567), never=3.67 (n=7459). ANOVA F=433, p=3.7e-187. Current smokers have ~0.62 months shorter PFS than former/never.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_023=1 (~65% of cohort) is associated with substantially shorter pfs_months than feature_023=0.
feature_011=1 (~25%) is associated with shorter pfs_months than feature_011=0.
feature_016=1 (~13%) is associated with longer pfs_months than feature_016=0 (a favorable feature).
feature_028=1 (~5%) is associated with shorter pfs_months than feature_028=0.
feature_018=1 (~35%) is associated with longer pfs_months than feature_018=0 (a favorable feature).

analyses.result_summary: feature_023=1: mean PFS 2.93 (n=32365); feature_023=0: 4.36 (n=17635). Mean diff=-1.44 months, p<1e-300. Strong negative prognostic.
feature_016=1: mean PFS 4.11 (n=6368); feature_016=0: 3.33 (n=43632). Diff=+0.78, p=7e-94. Favorable feature.
feature_018=1: mean PFS 3.64 (n=17544); feature_018=0: 3.32 (n=32456). Diff=+0.32, p=5.6e-49. Modest positive on average.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Several binary features (feature_002, feature_012, feature_020, feature_027, feature_021, feature_022) have negligible univariate association with pfs_months and are candidates for treatment-like indicators whose average effect is near zero (consistent with a treatment with strong heterogeneity, or a non-active feature).

analyses.code: for f in [feature_002, feature_012, feature_020, feature_027]: t-test vs pfs_months
analyses.result_summary: Univariate mean differences (treated minus untreated, p): feature_002 +0.009 (p=0.74), feature_012 -0.035 (p=0.08), feature_020 -0.025 (p=0.26), feature_027 +0.004 (p=0.87), feature_021 -0.190 (p=1e-17), feature_022 +0.189 (p=6e-11). feature_021 and feature_022 have small but significant main effects; feature_002, feature_012, feature_020, feature_027 are essentially null on average.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable OLS model adjusting for all features, feature_015, feature_014, feature_023, feature_011, feature_016, feature_018, smoking (feature_001), histology (feature_006), and feature_025/feature_017 will remain statistically significant predictors of pfs_months, while feature_002, feature_012, feature_020, feature_027, feature_021, feature_022 will lose their (already small) main effects.

analyses.result_summary: Model R^2 = 0.894. Significant adjusted coefficients (sign, p): feature_015 +0.168/yr (p<1e-300), feature_014 (level 1) -1.15, level 2 -2.18 (both p<1e-300), feature_023 -1.42 (p<1e-300), feature_011 -0.91 (p<1e-300), feature_016 +0.89 (p<1e-300), feature_018 +0.32 (p<1e-300), feature_001 former +0.55, never +0.55 vs current (both p<1e-300), feature_006 squamous -0.72 (p<1e-300), feature_025 +0.46/unit (p<1e-300), feature_017 -0.073/unit (p<1e-300), feature_032 -0.0004/unit (p<1e-25). feature_002, feature_012, feature_020, feature_021, feature_022, feature_027 all lose significance after adjustment (|coef|<0.02, p>0.1).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among all binary features tested as candidate treatments, the strongest treatment-by-feature interaction in predicting pfs_months will be feature_018 x feature_016: feature_018 will increase pfs_months substantially in feature_016=1 patients but have little effect in feature_016=0 patients.

analyses.code: for tx in binaries, mod in binaries: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ base + C(tx)*C(mod)').fit()
analyses.result_summary: Largest pure interaction term in adjusted screen is C(feature_016)[T.1]:C(feature_018)[T.1] = +2.53 (p<1e-300). Stratified test: feature_016=0 (n=43632) feature_018 mean diff = -0.005 (p=0.80); feature_016=1 (n=6368) feature_018 mean diff = +2.55 (p=1.7e-202).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the feature_016=1 subgroup, the treatment effect of feature_018 on pfs_months is suppressed by feature_031=1: PFS gain from feature_018 is large (>4 months) when feature_031=0 but ~zero when feature_031=1.

analyses.result_summary: Three-way interaction C(feature_018):C(feature_016):C(feature_031) = -4.69 (t=-39.2, p<1e-300). Stratified treatment effect within feature_016=1: feature_031=0 -> +4.64 months (n=1237 vs 2271, p<1e-300); feature_031=1 -> -0.01 months (n=1009 vs 1851, p>0.5). feature_031=1 fully abrogates the treatment benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the feature_016=1, feature_031=0 subgroup, feature_028=1 further abrogates the feature_018 treatment effect on pfs_months: the benefit is large when feature_028=0 and ~zero when feature_028=1.

analyses.code: Stratify feature_016=1 & feature_031=0 by feature_028; t-test feature_018 effect in each
analyses.result_summary: Within feature_016=1 & feature_031=0: feature_028=0 -> feature_018 effect = +4.83 months (n=1191 vs 2179, p<1e-300); feature_028=1 -> -0.29 months (n=46 vs 92, ns). 3-way model also confirmed independent attenuation by feature_028 (3-way coef -2.89, p=4e-21).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the feature_016=1, feature_031=0, feature_028=0 subgroup, feature_005=1 further abrogates the feature_018 treatment effect on pfs_months.

analyses.code: Within feature_016=1 & feature_031=0 & feature_028=0: stratify by feature_005
analyses.result_summary: Within feature_016=1 & feature_031=0 & feature_028=0: feature_005=0 -> feature_018 effect = +4.99 (n=1154 vs 2112, p<1e-300); feature_005=1 -> +0.05 (n=37 vs 67, ns). feature_005=1 also fully suppresses the treatment effect, consistent with another resistance/exclusion marker.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Define a 'responder' subgroup as feature_016=1 AND feature_031=0 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_005=0. The feature_018 treatment effect on pfs_months is large (~+5 months) within this subgroup and essentially zero outside it.

analyses.result_summary: Responder prevalence 6.53% (n=3266). Within responders: feature_018=1 mean PFS=8.23, feature_018=0 mean=3.25, diff=+4.99 (t=61.8, p<1e-300). Outside responders: feature_018=1 mean=3.32, feature_018=0 mean=3.32, diff=-0.006 (p=0.76). Interaction term coef=+4.99, p<1e-300.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the responder subgroup (feature_016=1, feature_031=0, feature_028=0, feature_005=0), no remaining binary or categorical feature further modifies the feature_018 treatment effect.

analyses.code: Within responder subgroup, for each remaining binary/cat feature, compute treatment effect at each level; flag if difference between strata >1.0 month.
analyses.result_summary: No remaining binary feature produced a stratified treatment-effect spread >1.0 month within the responder subgroup. Treatment effect by histology: adeno +5.09, squamous +4.85; by smoking: current +4.76, former +5.21, never +4.32; by feature_015 quintile: +4.60 to +5.16. Effect is essentially homogeneous within the responder definition.

proposed_hypotheses.text: No other binary feature in the dataset (besides feature_018) shows a clinically meaningful treatment-effect heterogeneity pattern of similar magnitude.

analyses.code: Triple-interaction screen across all (tx, bm, mod) triples of binary features, flagging |3-way coef|>1.0 with p<1e-15
analyses.result_summary: Only two triples emerged with |3-way coef|>1.0 and p<1e-15, both involving feature_018: feature_016 x feature_018 x feature_031 (3-way coef -4.69) and feature_016 x feature_018 x feature_028 (3-way coef -2.90). No other triple involved a different putative treatment. Stratified subgroup screens for feature_002, feature_020, feature_027, feature_021, feature_022, feature_012 also produced no subgroup with treatment-effect spread >0.3 months. feature_018 is the only treatment-like indicator with substantial heterogeneous effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_023=1 attenuates the positive slope of pfs_months on feature_015: the increment in PFS per unit feature_015 is smaller in feature_023=1 patients than in feature_023=0 patients.
feature_011=1 attenuates the positive slope of pfs_months on feature_015 relative to feature_011=0.

analyses.result_summary: Slope of pfs on feature_015: feature_023=0 -> 0.178, feature_023=1 -> 0.165. Interaction coef = -0.0126 per unit feature_015 (t=-10.4, p=2e-25). Significant prognostic interaction.
Interaction coef C(feature_011)[T.1]:feature_015 = -0.0114 (t=-7.84, p=5e-15). Slope is smaller in feature_011=1 patients. Significant prognostic interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the feature_001='never' subgroup, feature_018 has no detectable effect on pfs_months (consistent with the very low prevalence of the favorable biomarker feature_016 in never-smokers).

feature_018 has a positive but small main effect within both adenocarcinoma and squamous histology, but in both cases the effect is essentially driven by the responder subgroup (feature_016=1, feature_031=0, feature_028=0, feature_005=0).

analyses.result_summary: feature_018 effect by smoking: current +0.51 (p=6e-36), former +0.30 (p=3e-24), never +0.014 (p=0.78). Never-smokers show no benefit, consistent with their very low prevalence of feature_016=1 (2.0% vs 11.9% former, 19.7% current).
feature_018 effect: adenocarcinoma +0.28 (p=2e-27); squamous +0.45 (p=2e-28). Both modest in raw means, but the interaction with the responder definition is what carries the signal in each.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the continuous covariates outside feature_015, only feature_025 (positive) and feature_017 (negative) and feature_032 (slightly negative) have material independent association with pfs_months after adjusting for the strong predictors. The remaining continuous features (feature_019, feature_024, feature_010, feature_003, feature_030, feature_008, feature_004, feature_013, feature_026, feature_029, feature_007, feature_033, feature_009) do not.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS coefficients from full multivariable model
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted continuous-coef (effect per unit, p): feature_025 +0.463 (p<1e-300), feature_017 -0.073 (p<1e-300), feature_032 -0.0004 (p=2e-28). All other continuous features had |coef| trivial and p>0.10. feature_025 and feature_017 are the only meaningful additional continuous prognostic markers.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL: Treatment with feature_018 increases pfs_months by approximately +5.0 months exclusively in the subgroup defined by feature_016=1 AND feature_031=0 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_005=0; the effect is essentially zero in all other patients (so the average main effect of feature_018 of +0.32 months is entirely driven by this 6.5%-of-cohort responder subgroup).

analyses.code: Final responder model: pfs_months ~ C(feature_018)*C(responder); reaffirm with stratified t-tests
analyses.result_summary: Responder subgroup (n=3266, 6.53% of cohort): feature_018 effect = +4.99 months (8.23 vs 3.25, p<1e-300). Non-responder subgroup (n=46734): feature_018 effect = -0.006 (p=0.76). Interaction term in OLS = +4.99 (t=62.5, p<1e-300). Treatment-effect heterogeneity is fully captured by the four-feature subgroup definition; no further attenuation by histology, smoking, feature_015, or any other tested covariate.
